title: rinorea ovata
intro: Rinorea ovata, also known as the African poison ivy, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its shiny, green leaves and its ability to cause skin irritation in some individuals. The plant is rich in triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rinorea ovata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be used as a topical application or taken orally as a herbal remedy.
